name: Brown Butter Sablé with Tonka Bean Ganache
description: Crisp brown butter sablé cookies paired with a luxurious, aromatic tonka bean ganache for a balance of nutty, buttery crunch and creamy spiced chocolate.
keywords: brown butter, sablé, tonka bean, ganache, chocolate, pastry, dessert, fine dining

winePairings.name: Sauternes (half bottle)
winePairings.notes: Honeyed notes align with brown butter and vanilla.

winePairings.name: Tokaji Aszú 6 Puttonyos
winePairings.notes: Highly concentrated apricot sweetness matches richness.

steps.instruction: Pipe tonka bean ganache onto half of the sablés.
Top with a second sablé to form sandwich cookies or serve open-faced.
Garnish with cocoa nibs and a touch of gold leaf for an elegant finish.
Serve slightly chilled or at room temperature for the ideal texture contrast.
Melt butter in a saucepan over medium heat until it turns golden brown and releases a nutty aroma. Cool slightly.
In a mixing bowl, combine flour, almond flour, powdered sugar, and salt.
Add brown butter, egg yolk, and vanilla; mix until dough just comes together.
Shape into a log, wrap in plastic, and chill for 1 hour.
Slice into ½-inch rounds and bake at 170°C (340°F) for 12–14 minutes until golden brown at edges.
Heat cream with grated tonka bean just to a simmer for 10 minutes.
Remove from heat and strain.  Allow to cool for full flavor infusion.
Reheat cream and pour over chopped chocolate. Let sit for 1 minute.
Stir gently from the center until smooth and emulsified.
Incorporate butter and a pinch of salt until glossy.
Chill until thickened enough to pipe or spread.
